name: Jesse Jackson Jr.
bio: Jesse Jackson Jr. is listed as a Democratic candidate in the 2026 IL-02 race. Local election coverage notes his return to electoral politics in this open-seat contest. (Source: Chicago Sun-Times voter guide, March 2026: https://chicago.suntimes.com/politics/elections/2026/voter-guide/candidates)

jacket.note: FEC candidate totals pending ETL import.

red_flags.label: Federal conviction — campaign finance fraud (2013)
red_flags.detail: Jesse Jackson Jr. pleaded guilty in 2013 to federal charges of fraud and filing false tax returns. He misappropriated over $750,000 in campaign funds for personal use, including luxury items and memorabilia. Sentenced to 2.5 years in federal prison. This is a matter of public court record, not an allegation.

career_history.highlight: Resigned in 2012 amid federal corruption investigation
Served 2.5 years for campaign finance fraud

policy_platform.position: No current policy platform confirmed from public sources as of March 2026. Any candidacy in 2026 is not confirmed.

social_pulse.summary: Controversial public figure. Primarily known for 2012 federal corruption conviction. Any political comeback would face significant scrutiny.

trust_indicators.label: Resigned from Congress during federal investigation
Disclosure filings status
trust_indicators.value: Not confirmed in public sources as of March 2026
